process_name: Inclusive b -> s ell+ ell- rare B decay

evidence: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 as modern phenomenology systems.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:166 notes that the modern phenomenology fence is policy-only and not a numeric backend.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 implements check_mu_to_e_gamma; no b -> s ell ell observable is present in flavorConstraints.
Fixed-string targeted searches for b->s, b -> s, X_s, ell+ ell-, l+ l-, C_9, C_10 returned no matches in the required code directories.
commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -F -e 'b->s' -e 'b -> s' -e 'X_s' -e 'ell+ ell-' -e 'l+ l-' -e 'C_9' -e 'C_10' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(B\to X_s\ell^+\ell^-)\).
This entry covers the inclusive rare electroweak-penguin process
\(B\to X_s\ell^+\ell^-\), equivalently inclusive \(b\to s\ell^+\ell^-\),
with \(\ell=e,\mu\).  It excludes the exclusive modes \(B\to K\ell\ell\) and
\(B\to K^*\ell\ell\), and the LFU ratios \(R_K\) and \(R_{K^*}\), which are
separate catalog entries B016--B019.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical sidecar value is the HFLAV December 2024 inclusive average,
\[
  \mathcal B(B\to X_s\ell^+\ell^-)=(5.84\pm0.69)\times10^{-6},
\]
with the same table listing the PDG value
\((5.84^{+1.31}_{-1.23})\times10^{-6}\).  HFLAV combines the BaBar input
\((6.73^{+0.70}_{-0.64}{}^{+0.60}_{-0.56})\times10^{-6}\) and the Belle input
\((4.11\pm0.83^{+0.85}_{-0.81})\times10^{-6}\); the source notes different
low-dilepton-mass thresholds and different charmonium treatments in
\texttt{references/B015/hflav\_dec2024\_B\_to\_Xsll.txt}.  For the standard
perturbative bins, Huber--Hurth--Lunghi quote weighted experimental averages
of \((1.58\pm0.37)\times10^{-6}\) for \(1<q^2<6\,\mathrm{GeV}^2\) and
\((0.48\pm0.10)\times10^{-6}\) at high \(q^2\), as recorded in
\texttt{references/B015/huber\_hurth\_lunghi\_1503\_04849\_arxiv.txt}.
sections.relevance:
This is a \(\Delta B=1\) semileptonic FCNC channel.  It probes flavor-changing
\(Z\) couplings, electroweak-penguin matching, and dipole correlations with
\(b\to s\gamma\), rather than the \(\Delta F=2\) four-quark operators already
used by the repo's neutral-meson constraints.  In RS/anarchic flavor it is most
useful as a diagnostic of semileptonic Wilson coefficients such as \(C_7\),
\(C_9\), \(C_{10}\) and their chirality-flipped partners.
sections.post_2008:
Since the CFW 2008 RS-flavor baseline, the inclusive channel has gained a
BaBar measurement and a modern HFLAV average, but it remains statistics-limited
and experimentally based on sum-of-exclusive reconstruction.  Theory has also
matured: the 2015 Huber--Hurth--Lunghi analysis includes NNLO QCD, NLO QED,
power corrections, collinear-photon effects, and Belle II projections with
\(50\,\mathrm{ab}^{-1}\).  Current inclusive branching fractions are
compatible with the SM within uncertainties; the sharper exclusive and LFU
anomaly discussions belong in B016--B019.
sections.validity:
The inclusive perturbative windows are cleaner than exclusive form-factor
observables, but a catalog single number is not a plug-in hard bound.  The
interpretation depends on \(q^2\) binning, charm-resonance vetoes, photon
treatment, and the mapping from RS flavor parameters to the \(\Delta B=1\)
weak Hamiltonian.  It is best classified as a theory-controlled but
mode-calculation-limited rare-decay constraint.

In custodial RS, reduced \(Z b_L b_L\) pressure can make this observable
relatively more discriminating; quote RS bounds only after specifying
custodial protection, fermion embeddings, and brane kinetic terms.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The modern phenomenology policy enumerates only
\(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}, and states that this is
policy-only rather than a numeric backend at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:166}.  The only live lepton
flavor routine found is \(\mu\to e\gamma\) at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.  Required greps over
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no \(b\to s\ell\ell\),
\(X_s\ell\ell\), \(C_9\), or \(C_{10}\) implementation.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Production use would require a new \(\Delta B=1\) semileptonic
operator basis, matching and running for \(C_7,C_9,C_{10}\) plus primed
operators, bin-aware SM predictions, charm/QED treatment, and an experimental
likelihood or covariance model.
sections.key_references:
Process-local snapshots: HFLAV2024Dec:BtoXsll, BaBar2014:BtoXsll,
Belle2005:BtoXsll, HuberHurthLunghi2015:BtoXsll, and
CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor.
